review:
I love this game, but if you're considering purchasing it, it's important that you know what it is and what it is not. If you are looking for the kind of game with heavy player-animal interaction, this is probably not the game for you. If you want to do minimal management, this may not be the game for you. If you do not like design and construction (and I mean, a LOT of design and construction), this is not for you.

All that said, this is probably the best zoo simulator to ever exist. The amount of specificity, customization, realism, and detail is incredible, especially with architecture. Every animal has its own requirements and genetics. Guests and staff have a slew of different needs. You can map and track just about any metric possible. Years ago, I played Zoo Tycoon 2 extensively and was looking for a "new version," and this is a more than worthy successor. Some of the mechanics feel as if they were an answer to something I felt like Zoo Tycoon 2 lacked.

Not sure if you want something totally open-ended? You can still have a good time with it. I have exclusively played on career mode (and am trying a timed one). Currently, I have completed all base game career "levels" and am working my way through the DLC ones. Some of them involve taking over a pre-built zoo, some of them involve building a zoo from scratch, but you have to complete certain objectives to "win." I like having specific goals to work toward, and if that's the kind of thing you like, then you can still have fun. However, I think many players prefer sandbox, franchise, or challenge modes, which allow for even greater creativity.

Something to know is that the game does not give you much in the way of a tutorial. The tutorial is very barebones, and you have to figure out most of the complicated stuff yourself. Fortunately, people have made videos online that can help with this. Additionally, there are a lot of complicated tools and menus, and the construction process itself can be difficult and challenging to master. If you are the kind of person who will lose enjoyment in the game by seeing other players' intricate designs while you struggle to build a half-interesting enclosure, proceed with caution. But for me, I'm happy with my simpler designs and have slowly been learning more complicated things as I gain experience. Workshop blueprints others make can help with some of the complicated construction.

Of course, I have my minor gripes. The pathing is notoriously difficult, and terrain shaping can be hard. Animals can be finnicky, guests can be unreasonably whiny, and staff can be as smart as a rock. But these are small issues I can work around, and the fun I have designing and managing my zoos more than makes up for that. This game has pushed my creativity, and I notice and appreciate architecture, design, and zoos as a whole even more after playing this. And of course, I've learned quite a bit about different animals. Until Planet Zoo 2 comes out, I don't think you are going to find a more in-depth, customizable zoo simulator.

review:
因为喜欢各种动物，喜欢参观动物园，也喜欢模拟经营游戏，所以开始玩这款游戏。玩之前以为动物园只是给游客们提供一个参观全球各地物种的机会，没有什么动物保护的意识，所以接触到这款游戏中的动物福利、科普教育之类的要素觉得挺新奇，原来动物园还有这么多讲究。最近读了《红山动物园是我家》，里面有几段话，给了我很深的启发：
“现代动物园的职能不再是简单地满足游客的观赏需求，更像是保存野生动物基因的诺亚方舟，通过不断提高动物的福利、激发野性，从而实现物种的延续。动物园也不单单起到科普教育的作用，而是要做到更深层次的保护教育，不仅让人们认识到动物的生物学特征，还会让人们意识到在人、动物、自然三者关系中，不同的人分别可以做些什么，将人们保护野生动物的认知转化为实际行动。”
“动物是人类的朋友，它们代表野外的同伴来到城市，为人们带来欢乐、知识以及感悟。如果没有在动物园生活的它们，人们若想亲眼看到这些多姿多彩的生命要远行万里，这些 “大使” 的存在可以让人们用 1 天的时间进行一次环球旅行。它们以及它们的祖辈在来到动物园之后就很难再回到野外，我们要感谢这些生命，要努力让它们在动物园这个家中能有更多自然行为的表达，要让它们更快乐从容、更有自信、更有尊严地生活。”
“饲养员的工作不仅是人们口中 “给动物喂食” 那么简单，还有许多不为人知的艰辛 —— 他们需要花费许多的时间和精力去更好地照顾动物。这意味着他们在工作中要及时发现动物行为问题，思考该如何丰富动物的生活，如何才能使动物拥有更富有变化、挑战的生活。他们要带着探索与挑战的精神投入工作，思考如何给动物创造新的环境、新的刺激。总之，践行现代动物园建设这一价值理念，需要饲养员能做得更多。”
从此才了解到，现代动物园的工作人员并不是像我们养宠物那样对待动物，而是真正地为保护野生动物流汗甚至流血，身体力行地践行保护自然、保护生态环境的理念，比只会喊口号游行的所谓动物保护团体为动物、为人类做的多得多，向他们致敬。
顺便一说小熊猫是世界上最可爱的动物，建议每家分配一只，谢谢

review_english: I started playing this game because I like all kinds of animals, visiting zoos, and business simulation games. Before playing, I thought that zoos only provide tourists with an opportunity to visit species from all over the world, without any awareness of animal protection. So it was quite novel to be exposed to the animal welfare and popular science education elements in this game. It turns out that zoos have so many things to pay attention to. I recently read several passages in "Red Mountain Zoo is My Home" that gave me deep inspiration. The function of modern zoos is no longer to simply meet the viewing needs of tourists. It is more like Noah's Ark that preserves the genes of wild animals. By continuously improving the welfare of animals and stimulating wildness, the continuation of the species is achieved. The zoo does not only play the role of popular science education, but also provides deeper protection education. It not only makes people aware of the biological characteristics of animals, but also makes people aware of what different people can do in the relationship between humans, animals and nature. Transform people's awareness of protecting wild animals into practical actions. Animals are friends of humans. They come to the city on behalf of their companions in the wild, bringing joy, knowledge and insights to people. Without them living in the zoo, people would have to travel thousands of miles if they wanted to see these colorful lives with their own eyes. The existence of these ambassadors allows people to travel around the world in one day. It would be difficult for them and their ancestors to return to the wild after coming to the zoo. We want to thank these lives and strive to let them express more natural behaviors in the home of the zoo, so that they can be happier, calmer, more confident, and live with more dignity. The work of a keeper is not only as simple as feeding animals as people say, but there are also many unknown hardships. They need to spend a lot of time and energy to take better care of animals, which means they must discover animal behavior problems in time at work, think about how to enrich the lives of animals, and how to give animals a more varied and challenging life. They must go into work with a spirit of exploration and challenge, and think about how to create new environments and new stimulation for animals. In short, to implement the value concept of modern zoo construction, keepers need to do more. From then on, we learned that the staff of modern zoos do not treat animals like we keep pets, but truly sweat and even bleed to protect wild animals. Practicing the concept of protecting nature and protecting the ecological environment. They have done much more for animals and humans than the so-called animal protection groups that only chant slogans and parade. I salute them. By the way, the red panda is the cutest animal in the world. It is recommended that each family be allocated one. Thank you.

review:
The ultimate paradise for animal lovers and creative architects!

Rating: 👍 Recommended (10/10)

If you have ever dreamed of building your own zoo, forget about everything else. Planet Zoo by Frontier Developments is not just a regular simulator – it is an absolute masterpiece that has no competition in the tycoon genre. I have spent hundreds of hours in this game, and I am still fascinated by how much it has to offer.

🐘 Animals That Breathe with Life
What will completely blow you away in the first few minutes is the incredible detail of the animals. Every lion, elephant, penguin, or tiny chameleon looks and behaves like a living creature. Their fur, skin texture, realistic movement animations, and social interactions are simply breathtaking. Watching lion cubs play or bears swim in the water is an incredibly therapeutic experience. The animal AI is top-notch – they react to the environment, visitor noise, weather changes, and require complex care.

🏗️ Endless and Unmatched Building Options
If you love building, Planet Zoo is literally a digital Lego set with unlimited possibilities. The construction system is so deep and sophisticated that it can be mind-blowing. You can control literally every single piece of wood, stone, wall, or roof.

Terrain Deformation: Create massive caves, waterfalls, underground tunnels for guests, or hills covered with lush vegetation.

Piece-by-Piece Building: Don't want pre-made shops? Build your own modern restaurant, an African village, or a futuristic research center.

Botanical Paradise: A vast variety of plants, trees, and flowers from all over the world allows you to create a habitat so authentic that you’ll forget you are in a game.

The game gives you absolute freedom. If you have enough patience, you can create visual masterpieces that look like they belong in the real world. And if you don't feel like building everything from scratch, the Steam Workshop is filled with thousands of amazing creations from the community.

💼 Deep Management and Gameplay
The game is not just about looks; it’s also about brains. Franchise Mode is incredibly addictive. You manage the economy, cleanliness, staff happiness, disease research, animal genetics, and breeding programs so you can eventually release them back into the wild. The game has a wonderful educational aspect and puts a strong emphasis on ecology and conservation, which is highly commendable.

🎧 Audio-Visual Symphony
The graphics are beautiful, the lighting effects at sunset will take your breath away, and the soundtrack is so incredibly pleasant and relaxing that you’ll find yourself listening to it outside of the game. The animal sounds and the ambient noise of the zoo create perfect immersion.

Conclusion
Planet Zoo is a perfect game. It is a combination of deep management, unmatched creative freedom, and pure love for nature. If you love building strategies and animals, this is not a purchase you need to hesitate about. It is a must-have. 10/10

review: Офигенная графика, прикольно что можно наблюдать за животными. Но самым интересным для меня было разобраться наконец в принципах успешной игры. Да, есть гайды, но сразу после покупки почему-то не получалось даже по гайдам, сейчас вот получилось, думаю пройду игру до конца. Стадий довольно много, у меня каждая стадия занимает относительно много часов (возможно у кого-то заняло бы меньше, хз). Очень много деталей, есть возможность строить составные объекты (например, всякие лазилки для обезьян, но вроде можно строить и здания - я до этого еще не дошла). Очень радует русский перевод и русская озвучка. В общем, рекомендую :)

There are quite a few stages, for me each stage takes relatively many hours (maybe it would take someone less, I don’t know). There are a lot of details, it is possible to build composite objects (for example, all sorts of climbing frames for monkeys, but it seems that you can also build buildings - I haven’t gotten around to that yet). I am very pleased with the Russian translation and Russian voice acting. In general, I recommend)

review: I'm not recommending this game for one reason. You can't build how you want. The free build is a lie. Paths can only go in set directions. When you place paths and want to connect more paths to it, they wont connect half the time and when they do they wont connect how you want. Also if you have railings on and try to connect a path, the railings block the other path and you just have railings in the middle of your paths. When you try and place a blueprint, you can only place them in 1 meter intervals. This is the only game so far that i have had these problems in. I hope that this problem isn't in there next game.

review:
I’ve always loved animals, and I was disappointed when I couldn’t get into the biology and veterinary care class I wanted. Playing this game has honestly been a great alternative because it lets me learn more about the animals I love in a fun, interactive, and educational way. I enjoy discovering different species, learning about their needs and behaviors, and managing everything in a creative sandbox environment.
What makes the game even better is how relaxing and rewarding it feels to build and manage your own space while still learning new things along the way. You can tell that a lot of care went into making the animals feel unique and realistic, which makes the experience even more enjoyable for animal lovers like me.
It’s probably one of my favorite management games I’ve played, and I would definitely recommend it to anyone who loves animals, creativity, and sandbox or management-style games. Whether you want to learn, relax, or just spend time taking care of virtual animals, this game is absolutely worth trying.

review:
Pra quem amava ZooTycoon(1 ou 2) na infancia, esse jogo é perfeito! A liberdade de criação, a funcionalidade do "online"(troca de animais e visitas a zoos de outros players) e a variedade de especies(no base mesmo, sem falar de expansão), são incriveis.
Não joguei muito a campanha ainda, mas gostei da parte que joguei.
Único problema é que o jogo(no meu note) apresenta um erro vez ou outra(as vezes assim que abro o jogo, as vezes quando já estou jogando a algum tempo), não entendi ainda se é um problema de otimização ou algo da minha máquina. Mas, no geral, esse jogo me traz certa nostalgia, e me divirto jogando. Só queria conseguir jogar mais sem o jogo fechar por um erro inesperado :D

review_english: For those who loved ZooTycoon (1 or 2) as a child, this game is perfect! The freedom of creation, the "online" functionality (exchanging animals and visiting other players' zoos) and the variety of species (at the base itself, not to mention expansion), are incredible. I haven't played the campaign much yet, but I liked the part I played. The only problem is that the game (on my note) presents an error from time to time (sometimes as soon as I open the game, sometimes when I've been playing for a while), I still don't understand if it's an optimization problem or something with my machine. But overall, this game brings me some nostalgia, and I have fun playing it. I just wish I could play more without the game crashing due to an unexpected error D

review:
플래닛 시리즈중 제일 완성도 높은 게임.
그래픽 부분에서 정말 일체감있는 색감. 특히 비올때의 스모그 현상과 분위기는 정말 압도적이다. 빗물이 플러서 지붕을 타고 흘러내리는 연출까지. 컨텐츠가 풍성하고, DLC 또한 재미있다. 동물들의 행동이 살짝 반복적인 느낌이 없지만 그래도 현실에서의 동물의 모션을 정말 잘 표현했다. 

그래픽과 더불어 최적화 또한 완벽함. 1660SUPER 기준 높음 옵션에서 초반에 렉이 없었고, 맵 크기에 약 45%를 채울때까지도 렉이 느껴지지 않았다. 플래닛 시리즈중 제일 좋아하는 게임이다.

review_english: The most complete game in the Planet series. Really consistent colors in the graphics department. Especially when it rains, the smog phenomenon and atmosphere are truly overwhelming. Even the rain water flows down the roof. The content is plentiful, and the DLC is also fun. Although the animals' actions did not feel a bit repetitive, they still expressed the animal's motion in real life really well. In addition to the graphics, optimization is also perfect. There was no lag in the beginning with the high option based on 1660SUPER, and no lag was felt until the map size was filled to about 45. This is my favorite game in the Planet series.

review: My brother gifted me this game years ago so I felt like it was mandatory to give this game another try. But every time I do not play for a few weeks or months I forget every single detail about the micro managing in this game again and it just feels like a chore to read myself into how the mechanics work because they are not intuitive for me at all. I wanted to play this game for the animals but instead got a micro managing game that complicates everything. It is actually not enjoyable for me at all. Beyond that, the fact that I got the deluxe edition at release and then they still decided to lock extra content behind multiple paywalls is also not nice at all.

review:
Convoluted crap that rides on the back of overpriced DLC and a way-too-intricate building system. A sequel's coming soon, odds are it'll be a mess if Frontier's other recent releases are anything to go by. Dig for the old Zoo Tycoon games instead; they're not as visually impressive as Planet Zoo, but the actual gameplay in them is a hell of a lot more fun.

It takes a lot of effort to make the basic task of [i]placing an animal in the sandbox mode[/i] a tedious chore in your zoo game, but here we are.

review: I have had a good amount of time to form my opinion. I have zero faith in this company to make a functional game. The path system is insanely finicky, you could make an argument that it's actually broken. You are charged an arm and a leg for the base game plus all expansions for a game they do not ever bother to patch or fix. This is not a spiritual successor to Zoo Tycoon 2: Ultimate Edition, it is actually a very cheap and soulless imitation. I am so sad and disappointed they are also about to make this game completely obsolete because they are releasing a 2nd game! Get ready to have everything you do constantly break and bug your game while this company releases DLC after DLC and zero patches or acknowledgement of issues with gameplay. I truly hate this company. Basically just like EA, where they make the games good enough and just don't bother fixing bugs because they are so rich and popular that they don't need to care what people actually want.

review:
It's an addicting game and I love to play it. So why do I not recommend it? MONEY!!!

The base game is $45 even all these years after release. It has a good number of animals, scenes, props, etc but leaves a lot to be desired. Want more? No worries! Choose from any one of EIGHTEEN (18) different DLC, each one costing $10, and each one only providing a few new animals and a few new bits of scenery. In other words if you want the same level of freedom you had two decades ago with Zoo Tycoon, you have to shell out $220! Let that sink in...

I get it, the graphics are great, the interaction is fun, there's a lot more freedom of creativity, etc. etc. etc. But come on! More than $200 for a zoo game??? If half of the proceeds went to nature conservation that'd be one thing, but it doesn't. If you're truly obsessed with animals and zoos, buy the deluxe bundle on the best sale you possibly can. Otherwise don't bother.

review:
1300 hours later and I finally remembered I never wrote a review :D 
This game has something for everyone. Whether its being creative in sandbox and building hyper detailed zoos, running a chaotic franchise, or enjoying the career mode story. I spend most of my time building in sandbox creating semi-realistic or as I call it realistic-ish zoos. I'm always excited for a new DLC to drop so I can have new animals to build for and new building pieces to discover. Honestly has been money well spent. I MORE then got my money's worth. 
Only con it does have a steep learning curve because its so in depth. Whether its learning the controls, building mechanics, or management...it does take some time and sometimes some deep dive research through youtube. But once you get it...you GET IT and can enjoy the game.

review:
Empfehlung: Ja und Nein ... ich mach mal Nein?! Doch Ja?

Ich spiele seit Release und habe dem Spiel über die Jahre sehr viele schöne Stunden gegeben. Die Tiere sehen fantastisch aus, die Animationen sind liebevoll, Gehege zu planen macht unglaublich Spaß und wenn man erst einmal anfängt, Landschaften, Unterstände, Pflanzen, Felsen, Wege und Aussichtspunkte zu bauen, kann man komplett darin versinken.

Gerade das eigentliche Zoo-Gefühl trifft das Spiel oft extrem gut: Bedürfnisse prüfen, Pflanzen anpassen, Kletterbereiche kontrollieren, Unterstände bauen, Pflegerwege planen, Kackstellen erreichbar machen, Tier-DNA vergleichen, züchten, Naturschutzpunkte sammeln und sich dann über ein neues starkes Tier freuen. Das ist schon verdammt gutes Spieldesign.

Auch der Story-Modus war für mich immer ein Highlight. Die Basegame-Kampagne hatte Charme, schöne Ideen und Figuren, an die ich mich wirklich gerne erinnere. Das alte Ehepaar war grandios, und auch die spätere Entwicklung mit dem Bösewicht fand ich cool. Leider wurden die DLC-Kampagnen danach etwas dünner. ... generell finde ich die DLC, auch für die Preise etwas dünn.

Und dann kommt der Hass.

Das Wegesystem ist bis heute ein Pain in the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ass. Hügel bauen? Geil. Wege auf Hügel bauen? Viel Spaß im Wahnsinn. 
Klettertiere? Schön, bis man den Spielstand lädt und plötzlich wieder irgendein Kletterobjekt neu anfassen muss, damit das Tier seine Pfade wieder findet. 
Besucher hängen irgendwo fest. 
Menüs laden manchmal leer. 
Filter beim Tierkauf spinnen. 
Selbst für die Kampagne muss man online sein. 
Und das sechs Jahren nach release. Manche dieser Probleme einfach nicht mehr süß oder verzeihlich.

Besonders nervig ist auch der Workshop im Story-Modus: Die Community baut tausende wunderschöne Blueprints, aber das Game bietet keinen vernünftigen Filter für nicht freigeschaltete Inhalte. Viel spass scrollt euch halt nen wolf und seht den Wald vor lauter Bäumen nicht! Eine Wand aus Schlössern, nur um zwei nutzbare Objekte zu finden.

Beim Bauen liebe ich das Spiel, aber ich kämpfe ständig gegen Kleinkram: 
Warum kann ich Steine nicht frei skalieren? 
Warum fehlen feinere Grid-Optionen? 
Warum lassen sich viele vorhandene Story-Gebäude nicht bearbeiten oder kopieren? 
Warum drehen objekte wie Stützpfeiler nie passend zum grid?! 
Warum glitchen Tiere gelegentlich durch massive Wände, als wären sie spirituell erleuchtet?

Trotzdem: Die Optik ist süß, die Tiere sind fantastisch, die Vivarien als kleine Wimmelbilder finde ich klasse, automatische Tierverwaltung ist ein Segen, Kameras in Gehegen und zur Besucherüberwachung sind cool, und selbst gebaute Restaurants und Souvenirläden sind eine richtig starke Idee, danke für das Update Frontier! 

Planet Zoo ist ein wunderschönes, liebevolles, kreatives Zoo-Spiel, das sich leider regelmäßig selbst mit alten Bugs, sperrigen Systemen und unnötigem Quality-of-Life-Frust sabotiert.

Ich liebe es. Ich hasse es. Ich habe viel zu viele Stunden darin verloren.
Danke dafür Frontier, ich werde weiter unzählige Gehege mit voller Hingabe bauen. 

:D 

7/10

review_english: Recommendation yes and no... I'll do no?! But yes? I've been playing since it was released and have given the game many happy hours over the years. The animals look fantastic, the animations are lovely, planning enclosures is incredibly fun and once you start building landscapes, shelters, plants, rocks, paths and viewpoints, you can completely immerse yourself in them. The game often captures the actual zoo feeling extremely well: check needs, adjust plants, control climbing areas, build shelters, plan keeper paths, make poop spots accessible, compare animal DNA, breed, collect nature conservation points and then be happy about a new, strong animal. That's pretty darn good game design. The story mode was always a highlight for me. The base game campaign had charm, nice ideas and characters that I really remember fondly. The old married couple was great, and I also thought the later development with the villain was cool. Unfortunately, the DLC campaigns became a bit thinner after that. ... in general I find the DLC a bit thin, even for the prices. And then comes the hate. The trail system is still a pain in the ass today. Building hills? Cool. Build paths on hills? Have fun in the madness. Climbing animals? It's great until you load the save game and suddenly have to touch some climbing object again so that the animal finds its path again. Visitors are stuck somewhere. Menus sometimes load empty. Filters are crazy when buying animals. Even for the campaign you have to be online. And that’s six years after release. Some of these problems are just no longer sweet or forgivable. The workshop in story mode is particularly annoying. The community builds thousands of beautiful blueprints, but the game does not offer a reasonable filter for content that has not been unlocked. Have fun scrolling like a wolf and not seeing the forest for the trees! A wall of locks only to find two usable objects. When it comes to building, I love the game, but I'm constantly fighting small things. Why can't I freely scale bricks? Why are finer grid options missing? Why can't many existing story buildings be edited or copied? Why do objects like support pillars never rotate to match the grid?! Why do animals occasionally glide through solid walls as if they were spiritually enlightened? Nevertheless, the look is cute, the animals are fantastic, I think the vivariums as little hidden object pictures are great, automatic animal management is a blessing, cameras in enclosures and for monitoring visitors are cool, and self-built restaurants and souvenir shops are a really strong idea, thanks for the Update Frontier! Planet Zoo is a beautiful, loving, creative zoo game that unfortunately regularly sabotages itself with old bugs, clunky systems and unnecessary quality-of-life frustration. I love it. I hate it. I lost way too many hours in it. Thanks for this Frontier, I will continue to build countless enclosures with full dedication. D7 10

review:
There's a lot to do, a lot to learn and a lot to experiment with this game, and I'm only talking about the base version of it. If you are new as I was when I played PZ, you may find yourself a bit overwhelmed by everything. Start small, start slow. There's a ton of things to consider, from habitat distribution, coverage and special needs of animals, to guests needs and technical requirements such as energy and water, as well as staff workload efficiency. Animals have their own social, enviromental and dietary needs; as I said before, it's a lot for a beginner, but nonetheless fun once you get the grasp of it! I recommend watching some youtube videos for decorating the habitats. Don't forget to do reasearch with veterinaries and mechanics, as it will allow you to better the conditions of your animals, be better at disease management, and get a ton of new buildings and enrichment objects for the zoo. Also, put ATMs.

TLDR: very cool game, lots of things to consider, don't rush yourself into big habitats. Do reasearch to unlock new and necessary things for the zoo. Have fun!

review:
Parliamo di Planet Zoo. Il gioco che inizia con l'innocente idea di costruire uno zoo moderno e finisce con te che alle due del mattino stai modificando di tre gradi l'inclinazione di una roccia artificiale perché una tigre del Bengala ha deciso che il suo benessere è sceso dal 96% al 95%.
E sai una cosa? Ha ragione lei.

Estetica: la natura digitale più viva mai creata
Ci sono giochi con grafica migliore.
Ci sono giochi con animazioni migliori.
Ma pochi riescono a dare la sensazione di osservare esseri viventi come Planet Zoo.
Gli animali:
-si muovono credibilmente
-interagiscono tra loro
-giocano
-riposano
-si accoppiano
-crescono
-invecchiano
Non sembrano modelli tridimensionali.
Sembrano animali.

Gameplay: il paradiso dei maniaci del dettaglio
Planet Zoo ti offre due giochi contemporaneamente.
Il primo è un gestionale:
-economia
-personale
-ricerca
-riproduzione
-genetica
-conservazione
Il secondo è un simulatore di architettura ossessivo-compulsiva.
Puoi costruire praticamente qualsiasi cosa.
Un recinto.
Una cascata.
Un tempio cambogiano.
Un centro visitatori.
Una montagna artificiale.
Una voliera gigantesca.
Un'intera savana.
E inevitabilmente passerai quattro ore su qualcosa che i visitatori attraverseranno in sei secondi.

Gli animali: il cuore pulsante del gioco
La vera magia è qui.
Ogni specie ha:
-esigenze sociali
-esigenze ambientali
-comportamenti specifici
-requisiti climatici
-bisogni di arricchimento
Non basta mettere un animale in un recinto.
Devi capire l'animale.
E questa è una filosofia che traspare continuamente dal gioco.
Non stai gestendo oggetti decorativi.
Stai gestendo organismi.

Conservazione e biologia: finalmente trattate con rispetto
Una delle cose più belle è il messaggio di fondo.
Planet Zoo non glorifica la cattività.
Parla continuamente di:
-conservazione
-biodiversità
-benessere animale
-educazione ambientale
-gestione responsabile delle specie
Sotto l'aspetto gestionale c'è un sincero amore per la zoologia e per la tutela della fauna.
Una rarità in un medium che spesso usa gli animali come semplice decorazione.

Il vero nemico: te stesso
Il problema principale di Planet Zoo non è il gioco.
Sei tu.
Perché prima dici:
"Costruisco uno zoo semplice."
Poi:
"Potrei migliorare questo recinto."
Poi:
"Potrei fare una cascata."
Poi:
"Potrei ricreare un ecosistema tropicale completo."
Poi guardi l'orologio.
E sono passate cinque ore.

Difetti (perché perfino i panda hanno difetti)
Curva di apprendimento abbastanza ripida
Costruzione avanzata che può intimidire
Interfaccia a volte macchinosa
Richiede parecchio tempo per essere sfruttato davvero

Sintesi finale
Planet Zoo è probabilmente il miglior simulatore zoologico mai realizzato.
Non perché abbia più animali.
Non perché abbia più contenuti.
Ma perché riesce a trasmettere qualcosa che pochi giochi riescono a comunicare: il rispetto per la complessità della vita.
Ogni recinto diventa un ecosistema.
Ogni nascita una piccola vittoria.
Ogni specie una responsabilità.
E quando ti ritrovi fermo a osservare una famiglia di elefanti che interagisce nel recinto che hai costruito dopo ore di lavoro maniacale, capisci che Planet Zoo non parla di zoo.
Parla del fragile e straordinario miracolo della biodiversità.

review_english: Let's talk about Planet Zoo. The game that begins with the innocent idea of ​​building a modern zoo and ends with you at two in the morning changing the inclination of an artificial rock by three degrees because a Bengal tiger has decided that its well-being has dropped from 96 to 95. And you know what? She's right. Aesthetics the most vivid digital nature ever created There are games with better graphics. There are games with better animations. But few manage to give the sensation of observing living beings like Planet Zoo. The animals - move believably - interact with each other - play - rest - mate - grow - age They don't look like three-dimensional models. They look like animals. Gameplay: a paradise for detail freaks Planet Zoo offers you two games at the same time. The first is a management - economics - personnel - research - reproduction - genetics - conservation The second is an obsessive-compulsive architecture simulator. You can build almost anything. A fence. A waterfall. A Cambodian temple. A visitor center. An artificial mountain. A gigantic aviary. An entire savannah. And you'll inevitably spend four hours on something that visitors will go through in six seconds. Animals are the beating heart of the game. The real magic is here. Each species has -social needs -environmental needs -specific behaviors -climate requirements -enrichment needs It is not enough to put an animal in an enclosure. You have to understand the animal. And this is a philosophy that continually shines through the game. You are not handling decorative items. You are managing organisms. Conservation and biology finally treated with respect One of the most beautiful things is the underlying message. Planet Zoo does not glorify captivity. He continually talks about - conservation - biodiversity - animal welfare - environmental education - responsible management of species. From a management point of view there is a sincere love for zoology and the protection of fauna. A rarity in a medium that often uses animals as simple decoration. The real enemy yourself Planet Zoo's main problem isn't the game. It's you. Because first you say, "I'll build a simple zoo." Then "I could improve this fence." Then "I could make a waterfall." Then "I could recreate a complete tropical ecosystem." Then you look at the clock. And five hours have passed. Flaws (because even pandas have flaws) Fairly steep learning curve Advanced construction that can be intimidating Sometimes cumbersome interface Takes a long time to really get used to Final Summary Planet Zoo is probably the best zoo simulator ever made. Not because he has more animals. Not because it has more content. But because it manages to convey something that few games manage to communicate: respect for the complexity of life. Each enclosure becomes an ecosystem. Every birth a small victory. Every species has a responsibility. And when you find yourself standing still and observing a family of elephants interacting in the enclosure you built after hours of obsessive work, you understand that Planet Zoo is not about zoos. It's about the fragile and extraordinary miracle of biodiversity.

review: I love every aspect of this great game. The pathing could use some work, and I wish they had more animals, but aside from that I was extremely pleased with the entire game! The biggest frustration I have is that I play on a Steam Deck. It IS playable on a steam deck, but you will have to modify the controls in order to do so successfully, and fine tuning building structures is quite tricky. I was able to mostly circumvent this by downloading things from the workshop that others have created and tweaking them to my liking, since doing full builds of buildings and structures is difficult. However I have no trouble using premade structures or building enclosures.

Amazing game that is beautifully crafted, I find myself being drawn to this game anytime I am reminded of it. I love the premises of the game, create a zoo, design all of it while balencing beauty and funtionallity, get animals, raise them, breed them, and release them. Meanwhile teaching your guests about animals and earning money from them. My main issue with the game is that the controls take some getting used to and practicing with them. Another issue I have is the amount of DLCs there are, I play a ton of paradox games so it's not a big deal for me. Atleast unlike paradox the DLCs you don't need for gameplay, they are just props and new animals. Besides those two issues the game would be a 10/10.

Can't wait to play Planet zoo 2, I hope they keep the music that was created for the first game and I am looking forward to using wildlife reserves for my animals.

review:
Planet Zoo is easily one of the best zoo management games I've ever played.

Just like Planet Coaster, the amount of creativity and freedom you get is incredible. You can build a realistic zoo, create themed areas, design detailed habitats and make every zoo feel completely different from the last.

The animals are the real stars of the game. They look great, behave realistically and require proper care if you want them to thrive. Building habitats that fit their needs is just as important as making them look good for visitors.

There is also a strong management side to the game. You need to keep guests happy, manage your staff, balance your finances and make sure your animals are well cared for. It adds an extra layer to the game and makes your zoo feel like a real, living place.

The DLCs add even more animals, scenery and building pieces, giving you even more options to create the zoo you want.

Whether you enjoy building, management or simply love animals, Planet Zoo offers hundreds of hours of content and creativity.

Even after all this time, it's still a game I keep coming back to.

review:
Después de 112 horas, puedo decir que Planet Zoo es probablemente uno de los juegos de gestión más completos y detallados que he jugado.

Lo que más me gustó fue toda la parte de gestión y el realismo de los animales. No se limita a “poner animales bonitos y ya”, sino que realmente tienes que preocuparte por su bienestar, sus necesidades, las jerarquías sociales, la conservación de especies y hasta la genética. Se nota muchísimo el trabajo y el cariño que hay detrás de cada especie y toda la información educativa que incluye el juego.

También tiene un sistema de construcción muy bueno. Aunque personalmente juego más el modo carrera porque me gustan los retos y las misiones, me gusta que muchos mapas ya vengan parcialmente construidos para poder centrarme más en gestionar y mejorar el zoo sin tener que crear todo desde cero. Aun así, si eres creativo, puedes hacer auténticas barbaridades construyendo.

En cuanto a lo negativo, me pasó algunas veces que ciertos animales se escapaban o acababan en zonas donde los cuidadores no podían recuperarlos. No ocurre mucho y normalmente se evita haciendo bien los recintos, usando vallas adecuadas y evitando colocar árboles o superficies escalables cerca de ellas, pero puede ser algo frustrante cuando pasa.

Otro detalle importante es controlar la reproducción de animales de terrario, porque si no la limitas desde el principio, pueden multiplicarse demasiado rápido y acabar teniendo problemas de espacio. Pero una vez entiendes cómo funciona la gestión, deja de ser un problema.

En general, lo recomiendo muchísimo si te gustan los juegos de gestión, los animales o simplemente los juegos tranquilos donde puedes pasar horas mejorando poco a poco. Tiene una curva de aprendizaje, pero cuando entras en él, engancha muchísimo.

review_english: After 112 hours, I can say that Planet Zoo is probably one of the most complete and detailed management games I have ever played. What I liked the most was the whole management part and the realism of the animals. It is not limited to putting pretty animals and that's it, but you really have to worry about their well-being, their needs, social hierarchies, species conservation and even genetics. The work and love behind each species and all the educational information that the game includes is very noticeable. It also has a very good building system. Although I personally play the career mode more because I like the challenges and missions, I like that many maps come already partially built so I can focus more on managing and improving the zoo without having to create everything from scratch. Still, if you're creative, you can do some really amazing things building. As for the negative, it happened to me a few times that certain animals escaped or ended up in areas where the keepers could not recover them. It doesn't happen much and is usually avoided by making enclosures right, using proper fencing, and avoiding placing trees or climbable surfaces near them, but it can be frustrating when it happens. Another important detail is to control the reproduction of terrarium animals, because if you do not limit it from the beginning, they can multiply too quickly and end up having space problems. But once you understand how management works, it stops being a problem. Overall, I highly recommend it if you like management games, animals, or just quiet games where you can spend hours improving little by little. It has a learning curve, but when you get into it, it's very engaging.

review:
As a huge animal lover, Planet Zoo was an easy game for me to fall in love with. While it isn’t focused on directly interacting with animals, that’s because it’s first and foremost a management simulator and it does that incredibly well.
One of my favorite things is simply sitting back and watching the animals thrive while seeing visitors enjoy the zoo I’ve built. There’s something really satisfying about creating habitats, improving the park, and seeing everything come together. Sandbox mode is where I spent most of my time, and the freedom it offers is fantastic.
That said, Planet Zoo can be intimidating when you’re starting out. The building tools are incredibly powerful, but they can also feel overly complicated and overwhelming for new players. That’s my biggest hope for Planet Zoo 2 simpler and more intuitive building and management systems without sacrificing creativity.
I already pre ordered Planet Zoo 2 and I’m genuinely excited for it. Frontier has announced improvements along with new features like flying and aquatic animals, which makes the sequel even more exciting.  
My only real complaint is the DLC model. I own Planet Zoo and every DLC, and now I’ll be starting over with a new game and a new wave of DLC purchases to unlock additional animals. I understand why it happens, but it still stings as a long time fan.
Overall, Planet Zoo is one of the best zoo management games ever made. If you love animals, creativity, and building your dream zoo, it’s an easy recommendation.

review: Un super jeu !!! A chaque fois que je joue, j'ai une sensation de bien être !! je ne c'est pas comment l'expliquer mais avec la musique, les graphisme, et l'ambiance général on se sens hyper bien... On doit gérer son zoo de la construction, a la gestion, que se soit employer, ou animaux. Beaucoup de liberté de création, si on a de l'imagination. On peu construire des chose soit même ou le prendre dans le workshop. De beau graphisme, un gameplay un peu différent, car on a 3 mode de jeu : la carrière, la franchise et le mode bac à sable ( il y a les défis aussi ). Après pour moi y a 2 point noir. 1) le jeu a pas mal de petit bug, mais ça va sa casse pas non plus l'immersion ou le gameplay, mais sa casse les pieds, sa oui ! Et 2) trop de DLC payant et chère ! Dommage de pas avoir eu quelques DLC gratuit... Dans l'ensemble un très bon jeu. En espèrent qu'il corrige tout ça dans planète zoo 2 !

review_english: A great game!!! Every time I play, I have a feeling of well-being!! I don't know how to explain it but with the music, the graphics, and the general atmosphere we feel really good... We have to manage our zoo from construction, to management, whether it's employees, or animals. Lots of creative freedom, if you have imagination. You can build things yourself or take them from the workshop. Beautiful graphics, slightly different gameplay, because we have 3 game modes: career, franchise and sandbox mode (there are challenges too). Afterwards for me there are 2 black spots. 1) the game has a lot of little bugs, but it's not going to break the immersion or the gameplay, but it's going to be a pain, yes! And 2) too much paid and expensive DLC! Too bad not to have had some free DLC... Overall a very good game. Hopefully it fixes all that in Planet Zoo 2!

It's also great since it gives you real information about the animals; Where they are from, how many are at danger, and so much more.

Worth the money and you don't even need the DLC's to get a full experience out of the game.

review:
Game is very fun and full of content, just overwhelming at times! <3

I just got the game today but so far I can say it's pretty fun, there is lots of content and I have about 5 hours on the game so far. The information on the animals is great and if anyone wants to play a fun game that has some educational stuff about animals, this is a pretty good game for that. I love the models for the animals as well! Humans...not so much...   
The building is pretty fun! It is a little confusing at first and may be hard to navigate but as you make your way through the tutorials it gets easier. Everything else is pretty easy to navigate.
I got a few DLCs and I am excited to try those out and see more content, I think there is plenty of content without any DLCs though. 
Only thing I have to say is: if you get overwhelmed easily, this game can get pretty overwhelming. I have always come back though, it is not so overwhelming you wouldn't wanna just take a break and come back. So, I suggest getting this game if you like animals or want to learn more about animals, or enjoy tycoon like games in a more realistic setting.

review: This game is amazing for anyone who enjoys animals and creative games! There's so much stuff you can build and design, and it's not constrictive like a lot of sandbox games are. They give you many pieces to build with, and the game mechanics are great. One thing I will say is that some of the animal designs are a bit rough or get boring after a while of seeing the same thing over and over. I wish the coat patterns and markings on certain animals were more diverse, or there was more variation in body build and things like that. Aside from that, this game is great, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fun, creative outlet that incorporates animals and conservation.

review: Amazing game, very good simulation, animals are great, dlc are fun too and add a lot of new animals and building blocks. The only downside is too complex build system that has no valid tutorial and you will have to learn how to play online on youtube videos or similar. I just don't understand the developers being so lazy to go through 3 scenarios with valid tutorial for all shortcuts, or at least putting these same shortcuts as icons in the menu with tips of what they do so we can explore on our own. The way it works is that you accidentally press something on keyboard and you have no idea what it does and how to get back, so you have to watch tutorials. The game build system is superrealistic though and if we forget the fact that they didn't explain how to use their build system, you can really make a lot of realistic zoo content from all around the world, the effects are great too, the animal animations are fun. The community is still rich so you can find a lot of players online daily.

review:
I wrote out so much and accidently deleted it, so Ill rewrite it in a shorter way.
This game is incredible, but new players should definitely wait for discounts before blowing money away. The DLC's make the game so much more entertaining, there is so much you can create. The animals are incredibly realistic, but i do think the older ones/ most of the ones that come with the game no DLC's could use some work. The pathing and building can be annoying sometimes, but other then that it is pretty easy. I love the natural habitats you can make, the nature options are very broad There's sandbox mode and normal mode so you don't have to worry about costs and just free roam the game. I think the terrain can be annoying, depending on where you are is how the terrain will look. Its a fun and relaxing game, i definitely recommend it.

review:
I came to this game after playing their older title, Zoo Tycoon, hoping for more freedom, more animals, and deeper customization and honestly, this feels like the perfect evolution of that idea. The game gives you an incredible amount of creative freedom and attention to detail, allowing you to build your dream zoo with almost no restrictions, which is exactly why it works so well.
If you’re thinking about buying it, I’d definitely say it’s worth the price. However, keep in mind that building and designing your zoo is a huge part of the experience. If you prefer a more casual zoo game with minimal management or you’re not really into spending time building, this might not be the game for you.
I also own all of the DLC packs. They’re absolutely not necessary to enjoy the game, but they add so much extra content; unique animals, new challenges, additional building pieces, and more creative possibilities. I’d highly recommend them as well, especially during sales when you can grab several at once for a great price.
They really did an amazing job with this game. Definitely recommend it.

I’m a big fan of tycoon games and have played a bunch of them. I don’t usually write reviews, but I just had to for this one.

“This is how tycoon games should be made.”

Honestly — this is probably the most complex and best-made tycoon game ever released. It’s got a level of depth you don’t really see anywhere else, but it’s still fun and keeps you awake the whole time.

review:
I don't know why I waited so many years to play this game.
I have been playing simulation games for three decades. This is the best one I've played yet!
54 hours in one week- I'm hooked. In that 54 hours I encountered no glitches, bugs, or crashes. 
Tired of managing the business side? Switch over to scenery and spend hours landscaping. Tired of that? Switch over to caring for animals. There's so many aspects to this game there is no way to get bored easily. Highly impressed.

review:
Planet Zoo é um jogo simplesmente lindo! A parte de construir os habitats decorar o zoológico e depois ficar observando os animais vivendo no ambiente que você criou é muito relaxante e satisfatória. Dá pra perceber o carinho nos detalhes e na ambientação principalmente no comportamento dos animais que deixa tudo muito imersivo.

Ao mesmo tempo achei o jogo detalhado até demais em alguns momentos. O nível de realismo é impressionante mas para quem joga de forma mais casual como eu, acaba ficando um pouco cansativo e as vezes até chato. São muitos pequenos detalhes para administrar o bem estar dos animais e dos visitantes. Mesmo assim, dá pra entender totalmente quem ama esse lado mais complexo e realista porque o jogo entrega isso muito bem. Para jogadores mais hardcore de gerenciamento provavelmente é um ponto super positivo.

review_english: Planet Zoo is a simply beautiful game! The part of building the habitats, decorating the zoo and then watching the animals living in the environment you created is very relaxing and satisfying. You can see the care in the details and the ambiance, especially in the behavior of the animals, which makes everything very immersive. At the same time, I found the game too detailed at times. The level of realism is impressive but for those who play more casually like me, it ends up getting a little tiring and sometimes even boring. There are many small details to manage the well-being of animals and visitors. Even so, you can totally understand those who love this more complex and realistic side because the game delivers this very well. For more hardcore management players it's probably a super positive point.

review:
Less than half of the people who have bought and installed Planet Zoo have completed the first tutorial level. I’m not surprised. This isn’t a bad game, but it is so fiddly in a number of ways. 

I do understand this game would be more fiddly than Planet Coaster; after all, it is much harder to keep animals alive than it is to keep a roller coaster functioning. However, I wasn’t expecting so much crap do deal with. Most animals need hard shelter, which seems impossible to keep up with. You can have a herd of a dozen zebras, but you will need so much hard shelter than you will wonder how the species exists in nature. I have a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ hotel complex here and they are still complaining, so what the hell do zebras to in real life when it rains? Also, how does a tree called Umbrella Tree not create shelter? We should be told which trees create shelter and a LOT more of them should.

When filtering habitat items, they show EVERY species in the game and there’s no option to just show the species your zoo has. I don’t want to have to scroll through dozens and dozens of species just to get to Timber Wolves when that’s the only species I have in my zoo. 

The one-way glass has a 50/50 chance of going the wrong way and you have to manually flip it. Why would I EVER want to stop my customers from seeing the animals and not the other way around? The game should use the door to determine which side is which. 

I should be able to mass select items and then SELL THEM, not just delete them. I need the money, so if it would cost me money to remove it (mostly trees) then leave it there and I will move it for free. 

You can keep different species in the same habitat, and the game will tell you when these species will receive an interspecies enrichment bonus. However, that’s the only information you get. There’s no way to tell in game if two species are fully compatible, i.e., they won’t fight and their habitat needs overlap enough to be at 100%. Or even if they are semi-compatible, i.e., they won’t fight, but some of their habitat needs don’t mesh and you will need to compromise. This should absolutely be in the game. 

Another thing, while playing the campaign, you will often get gift animals and they are almost exclusively garbage. Furthermore, they are territorial and you can’t sell or release gift animals, so you are stuck with them till they die. “Thank you for this gift. I will put them in a habitat, but as soon as there’s another alpha male, I will stick them in storage where they will stay till they die.” This seems at odds with their pro-animal welfare message. 

And the worst part about this... The game is still really good. If they improved the UI a little and gave the users more information about compatibility and shelter than this would be an amazing game. As it is, I can see myself going back and playing again to get another 10 or so achievements, but I can never see myself putting in the effort to 100% the game.

review: Planet Zoo is one of the best management sims I’ve played. The level of detail in the animals, habitats, and zoo building is incredible, and it’s both relaxing and rewarding to grow your park over time. The customization options are deep without feeling overwhelming, and watching guests react to your zoo makes it feel alive. Perfect game if you enjoy creativity, strategy, and animals.

review:
The game by it self is fun and there is plenty of dlc around. The various interface and menus are overwhelming at first. Even tho i do recommend this game its economy is brutal, unforgiving and even stressful in a way that complicates matters, sure you can turn off most negative stuff and even money in sandbox mode, but u never learn the economy that way. Took me 8 or so testruns to even get to the point were the zoo was making money and then i had only butterfliefarm and stores, the second i added another new animal it was going minus again. 
Entryfee earns nothing
even tho understaffed wages eat up all your money
and animal food also takes alot
compared to other similiar games it makes no sence how idiotic the system work, for example almost highest rating, animal welfare and 3700 guests but u still do not make any money, best thing seems to make a zoo with no animals and only stores.....

Also as several others had wrote prior the pathing and snap function of the game is hardly working at all and you have to force a simple pathway, no logical sense how it ataches to other things

Entryfee earns nothing even tho understaffed wages eat up all your money and animal food also takes alot compared to other similiar games it makes no sence how idiotic the system work, for example almost highest rating, animal welfare and 3700 guests but u still do not make any money, best thing seems to make a zoo with no animals and only stores..... review: Very good game, taught me a lot about wildlife and even native plant life. While yes, there are paid DLC, they aren't required to have fun like in most games, and the game is still playable and fun without them. It caters to people willing to spend hours placing small details around every corner and crevice, and to those just wanting to conjure up a quick and easy enclosure with the barriers available in-game. The controls and building system make take a bit to get used to, so I recommend taking time in sandbox just figuring out all the mechanics or playing the tutorial before trying on your own. 11/10 game, 100% would recommend.

review:
Planet Zoo, hayvanat bahçesi kurma ve yönetme oyunları arasında gerçekten çok başarılı bir yapım. Oyunda sadece kafesler yapıp hayvanları yerleştirmiyorsunuz; her hayvanın yaşam alanı, sosyal ihtiyaçları, beslenmesi, iklim koşulları ve refah seviyesiyle ayrı ayrı ilgilenmeniz gerekiyor. Bu da oyunu hem daha gerçekçi hem de daha keyifli hale getiriyor.

Grafikler oldukça güzel, hayvan animasyonları çok detaylı ve özellikle hayvanların doğal davranışlarını izlemek gerçekten tatmin edici. Kendi hayvanat bahçenizi sıfırdan tasarlamak, yolları, dekorları, mağazaları ve habitatları düzenlemek çok fazla yaratıcılık sunuyor.

Başlarda menüler ve yönetim sistemi biraz karışık gelebilir ama alıştıktan sonra oyun oldukça bağımlılık yapıyor. Ziyaretçilerin mutluluğu, hayvanların sağlığı, çalışanların yönetimi ve park ekonomisi derken zamanın nasıl geçtiğini fark etmiyorsunuz.

Hayvanları, simülasyon oyunlarını ve detaylı tasarım yapmayı seven herkese kesinlikle tavsiye ederim. Planet Zoo hem rahatlatıcı hem de uzun süre oynanabilecek kaliteli bir oyun.

Puanım: 10/10
review_clean: Planet Zoo, hayvanat bahçesi kurma ve yönetme oyunları arasında gerçekten çok başarılı bir yapım. Kendi hayvanat bahçenizi sıfırdan tasarlamak, yolları, dekorları, mağazaları ve habitatları düzenlemek çok fazla yaratıcılık sunuyor. Hayvanları, simülasyon oyunlarını ve detaylı tasarım yapmayı seven herkese kesinlikle tavsiye ederim. Planet Zoo hem rahatlatıcı hem de uzun süre oynanabilecek kaliteli bir oyun. Puanım 10 10
review_english: Planet Zoo is a really successful game among zoo building and management games. In the game, you do not just make cages and place the animals, you have to take care of each animal's living space, social needs, nutrition, climate conditions and welfare level separately. This makes the game both more realistic and more enjoyable. The graphics are quite beautiful, the animal animations are very detailed and it is especially satisfying to watch the natural behavior of the animals. Designing your own zoo from scratch, arranging paths, props, shops and habitats offers a lot of creativity. The menus and management system may seem a little confusing at first, but once you get used to it, the game becomes quite addictive. You don't notice how time passes between the happiness of the visitors, the health of the animals, the management of the employees and the park economy. I definitely recommend it to anyone who loves animals, simulation games and detailed design. Planet Zoo is a quality game that is both relaxing and can be played for a long time. My score is 10 10

review:
I would normally recommend it, however, it is currently in an unplayable state (if you have some of the DLC's, it seems?)
For a minimum of 2 years now you can find threads and reports of people saying they can't open the animal trading at all, meaning no animals can be obtained whatsoever. That's game breaking for a zoo game where visitors and therefore money income only happen if you have animals, eh? 
I've tried old and new save files of Franchise and Career, it works in neither. I have Zoos with 5 Star ratings and an immense diversity of animals but can't get any new ones, while new Zoo's can't get any to begin with.
I've found 2 year old user reports of it on Frontier's own website, yet nothing is being done, probably to force higher traffic for Planet Zoo 2 considering that now people can't go "I will just play the first game until the new one is on sale" or something.

I won't be buying Planet Zoo 2 as long as this bug exists, since it tells me that the devs don't care about entire game demolishing issues.
